titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Mary Anne Curley, 19 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926) to Mary Anne Curley acknowledging Mrs. Curley's previous letter in regards to her children's placement in industrial schools.Sir Edward O'Farrell served as the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3730

correspAction.placeName: No. 3 Caseys Cottages, Little Bray, Co. Wicklow, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dublin Castle, 19th February, 1916 Sir Madam, I am directed by the Lords Justices to
acknowledge the receipt of your letter of the 13th instant. Dear Sir,
I have wrote to the Lord Lieutenant
to aquaint him about my four children which had been
taken and sent from me. I cannot tell there whereabouts
nor would I be let know, that I might go see them.
My husband is at the front we are both willing to
keep our children and has a good clean home for them
Dear sir, hoping your honour will deliver and help me
by telling me what to do in gaining my childrens
liberty
I am Dear Sir
Your obedient Servant
Mary Anne Curley
No. 3 Caseys Cottages
Little Bray I am,
Sir, Madam ,
Your obedient Servant, EOFarrell To Mrs. Mary Anne Curley, No. 3 Casey's Cottages, Little Bray. Co. Wicklow.
